title: Recall@K
description:
Compute Recall@K: the fraction of relevant items found in the top-K retrieved results.

`Recall@K = |relevant ∩ top-K| / |relevant|`

**Example:**
```
relevant  = {1, 3, 5}
retrieved = [3, 2, 1, 4, 5]
Recall@3  = 2/3 ≈ 0.667
```
constraints: Return 0.0 if no relevant items exist
retrieved is an ordered list (rank 0 = best)
starter_code:
def recall_at_k(relevant: set, retrieved: list, k: int) -> float:
    """Compute Recall@K.

    Args:
        relevant: Set of relevant item IDs
        retrieved: Ordered list of retrieved item IDs
        k: Cutoff rank

    Returns:
        Recall@K as float in [0, 1]
    """
    pass
explanation: Recall@K measures how many of the truly relevant items appear in the top-K returned results. It is important when the cost of missing a relevant item is high (medical diagnosis, search). Unlike Precision@K, it accounts for the total pool of relevant items.
hints: Convert top-K to a set for O(1) lookup
Divide intersection size by total relevant count
